---
artifact_id: ESE111-CH04-EXP01
pair_id: ESE111-CH04-EXP01
lang: en
pair_revision: 0.1.0
semantic_id: ESE111-CH04-EXP01
id: ESE111-CH04-EXP01-en
resource_type: guided-exploration
language: en
paired_with: ESE111-CH04-EXP01-fr
version: 0.1.0
lifecycle: drafting
outcomes: [ESE111-CO01, PLO-01, PLO-07]
estimated_student_time_min: 35
safety_level: L1
license: CC-BY-SA-4.0
technical_contract:
  outcomes: [ESE111-CO01, PLO-01, PLO-02, PLO-07, PLO-08]
  equations: [KCL signed current sum equals zero, KVL signed loop voltage sum equals zero, v equals R times i, power equals v times i]
  values: {source_v: 6.0, r1_ohm: 10000, r2_ohm: 22000, r3_ohm: 33000, r4_baseline_ohm: 15000, r4_redesign_ohm: 22000}
  safety_limits: {source_max_v: 6.1, current_limit_max_a: 0.02, dmm_current_input_permitted: false}
  expected_ranges: {baseline_tp_b_simulated_v: [1.087, 1.155], redesign_tp_b_simulated_v: [1.412, 1.500], source_current_simulated_a: [0.000229, 0.000244]}
  rubric_total: 28
  answer_key_digest: public-formative-key-v0.1.0
  figure_ids: [ESE111-CH04-FIG-001]
---

# Exploration — Can observing TP-B change TP-B?

Predict whether a finite-resistance voltmeter raises, lowers, or leaves TP-B
unchanged. Give a mechanism, not a guess. This 35-minute exploration may use the
authorized physical network or the deterministic model; only the physical route
can evidence probe connection.

Use the LAB04 safety envelope and stop conditions. In the model, hold every value
fixed and compare no meter, 10 MΩ, 1 MΩ, and 100 kΩ at TP-B. Record TP-B, meter
current, and the KCL-B residual when meter current is deliberately omitted. Plot or
tabulate input resistance on a logarithmic scale; label provenance.

Questions progress from description to transfer:

1. What changed monotonically, and what limiting value appears as (R_M\to\infty)?
2. Why does the omitted-branch KCL residual equal meter current apart from rounding?
3. At what (R_M) does loading exceed 1% of the unloaded TP-B value?
4. Would adding display digits reduce loading? Which specification matters instead?
5. Design one change to the *measurement route*, not the network, that reduces
   loading while preserving the question. State its acceptance test.

The expected nominal observations are 1.1276 V unloaded and 1.1154 V at 1 MΩ.
Those are analytical values, not apparatus pilot data. Submit prediction, command/
configuration, recoverable table, one equation, loading decision, and one limit.
